dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4342 IS: To extend section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 for 18 months.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Grassley (for himself and Mr. Cotton) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Select Committee on Intelligence

form.official-title: To extend section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 for 18 months.

section.header: Extension of section 702 authority for 18 months

subsection.text: Section 403(b) of the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 (Public Law 110–261; 122 Stat. 2474) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (1) (50 U.S.C. 1881 note), by striking two years after the date of enactment of the Reforming Intelligence and Securing America Act and inserting on October 20, 2027; and

in paragraph (2) (18 U.S.C. 2511 note), in the matter preceding subparagraph (A), by striking two years after the date of enactment of the Reforming Intelligence and Securing America Act and inserting on October 20, 2027.

subsection.text: Section 404(b)(1) of the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 (Public Law 110–261; 122 Stat. 2476), is amended, in the heading, by striking two years after the date of enactment of the Reforming Intelligence and Securing America Act and inserting October 20, 2027;
